title: Finger ECG-based Authentication for Healthcare Data Security Using Artificial Neural Network
venue: 2017 IEEE 19th International Conference on e-Health Networking, Applications and Services (Healthcom)

method.input_representation: Difference vectors of 7 features (Euclidean distance of QRST/QRS segments, Manhattan distance of R amplitude, and kurtosis of QRST/QRS segments) compared against a user template
method.training_strategy: Two-phase authentication using a 'General' NN model for preliminary screening (population-based) followed by a 'Personal' NN model for fine-grained identification (individual-based).

evaluation.context: Average result for sample size of 5 users
Average result for sample size of 5 users

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-out (LOO) cross-validation repeated 100 times for various subset sizes (5, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50).

reported_limitations: Performance decreases as the number of users increases
Kurtosis of QRS/QRST patterns are less distinguishable between groups

key_contribution: Two-phase authentication combining a general population model and individual personal models
Reduced authentication time to 3 heartbeats (~3 seconds)
main_strength: Evaluated under different physical statuses (pre- and post-stress test)
main_weakness: Poor scalability (error rates increase with population size)
Only tested in a controlled lab environment
insights.notes_for_review: The paper claims this is the shortest authentication time reported for ECG to date.
